frameworks.name: multivariate pattern classification
frameworks.one_line: decode distributed brain activation patterns into discrete emotion categories

frameworks.name: affective neuroscience category models
frameworks.one_line: extends evoked-emotion decoders to spontaneous resting-state activity

core_question: Can neural classifiers trained on evoked emotions decode spontaneous emotional states from resting-state brain activity?
key_assumptions: distinct emotion categories have separable distributed neural signatures
classifiers trained under emotion induction generalize to unprovoked states
self-reported feelings index ongoing emotional experience
manipulations: evoked emotion induction vs. resting state
two independent experiments testing predictive validity
individual differences in mood and personality traits
key_findings: resting-state activity decodes into distinct emotion categories
decoded states covary with mood and personality traits
decoded states predict online self-reported feelings
interpretations: validates objective brain-based models of emotion categories
emotional states emerge dynamically from separable neural systems
